title: Linear Programming and Constrained Optimization · Guided Modeling Studio
summary: Choose a fair production and distribution plan under limited resources. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of an explainable constrained optimization model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define decision variables, objective, constraints, units, and feasible domain.
Solve two-variable linear programs graphically and verify corner candidates.
Encode a constrained model for a solver and independently validate its solution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

content.text: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (6 × 9) − (1 × 2).
